3. The State can regulate any economic or political activity associated with religious practice.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Secularism safeguards freedom of religion or belief and ensures that the government does not favour any particular religion or impose religion on its citizens.
● Indian secularism took on a distinct form as a result of an interaction between what already existed in a society that had religious diversity and the ideas that came from the West. It resulted in an equal focus on intra-religious and inter-religious domination.
○ Secularism is a doctrine that opposes any form of religious and intra-religious domination. It promotes freedom within religions and promotes equality between, as well as within religions. Thus, Article 15 of the Constitution of India mentions that the State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth or any of them. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Indian secularism does not focus only on Church-State separation. Since a secular State must be concerned equally with intra-religious domination, Indian secularism has made room for and is compatible with the idea of State-supported religious reform. Thus, the Indian Constitution bans untouchability. Article 25 (2) (b) states that the State can opt for social welfare and reform or the throwing open of Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all classes and sections of Hindus. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Indian secularism deals not only with the religious freedom of individuals but also with the religious freedom of minority communities. Within it, an individual has the right to profess the religion of his or her choice. Likewise, religious minorities also have a right to exist and to maintain their own culture and educational institutions. Article 25(1) states that all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practice and propagate religion.
● Article 25(2)(a) states that the State can regulate or restrict any economic, financial, political or other secular activity which may be associated with religious practice. So, statement 3 is correct. So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: Various Central Banks around the world including the Reserve Bank of India have increased their holding of Gold Reserves.
Statement II: Gold is a zero yield investment.
Statement III: Gold acts as a hedge against currency fluctuations.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
● The share of gold in the total foreign exchange reserves of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) increased to about 11.70% by March 2025, from 9.32% as at the end-of September 2024 and 5.87% as at the end-of March 2021.
● As of end-March, the RBI held 879.59 metric tons of gold, compared to 854.73 metric tonnes at the end of September. The proportion of gold held domestically rose to 511.99 metric tonnes as of March 31, against 510.46 metric tonnes at September-end. So, statement I is correct.
● Gold is globally recognized as a safe-haven asset, especially during times of economic uncertainty, currency volatility, and inflation. Gold reserves act as a hedge against currency fluctuations because they are considered a stable store of value, independent of any particular currency or economic performance. When currencies fluctuate due to inflation, economic uncertainty, or geopolitical instability, gold tends to retain or even appreciate in value, acting as a safe haven asset. So, statement III is correct.
● Gold does not generate interest or dividends, unlike bonds or equities, hence it is a zero yield investment. Its value appreciates primarily through capital appreciation, which is subject to market sentiment and may fluctuate. So, statement II is correct. So, Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following are applications of the geotubes:
I. Wastewater treatment
II. Prevention of coastal erosion
III. Dredging of water bodies
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● Geotubes are large, permeable fabric tubes filled with dredged materials or other hydraulically filled materials. The geotextile fabric allows water to escape while retaining the solid particles, making them effective for dewatering purposes and retaining soil from erosions.
● Geotubes are extensively used in wastewater treatment, particularly for dewatering sludge. They provide a cost-effective method to reduce sludge volume, facilitating its disposal or reuse. So, point I is correct.
● One of the most prominent applications of geotubes is in coastal and shoreline protection. They help prevent coastal erosion by acting as barriers against wave action and stabilizing the shoreline. So, point II is correct.
● Dredging is the process of extracting sediment and debris from the beds of waterways.The dewatering stage of dredging involves removing water from the sandy or rocky sediment so only solid particles remain. A pump within the body of water being dredged hydraulically or mechanically fills the geotube with dredged material. This material is commonly known as sludge or slurry and consists of water mixed with sand or dirt, depending on the type of waterway. Once inside the geotextile tube, the sludge is treated with environmentally-safe polymers via a polymer-injection system. These agents speed up the dewatering process by binding the solids together and separating them from the water. So, point III is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to S-400:
I. It is an anti-aircraft defence system developed by Israel.
II. It is fitted with a long-range Surface-to-Air Missile (SAM).
III. It can detect targets at a range of up to 1000 km.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Explanation:
● The S-400 missile system, is one of the world’s most advanced anti-aircraft defence system fitted with long-range surface-to-air missile (SAM) systems developed by Russia’s Almaz-Antey. India signed a contract for five S-400 units in 2018, significantly enhancing its air defence capabilities. So, statement I and statement II are correct.
● The S-400 system features highly sophisticated radar technology capable of detecting targets at a range of up to 600 km, which exceeds the maximum missile engagement range of about 400 km (achieved by the 40N6 missile variant). This means the radar can detect threats well beyond the missile’s maximum firing range, allowing early warning and better target tracking. So, Statement III is not correct.
● The S-400 system is designed for rapid deployment and high mobility. It is mounted on wheeled vehicles, enabling quick “shoot and scoot” operations. The system can be set up in 5 to 10 minutes and relocated swiftly after firing to avoid counterattacks. This mobility makes it a versatile anti aircraft defence system.
● The missiles used in the S-400 system are capable of reaching speeds up to Mach 14 (around 17,000 km/h), which is well within the supersonic and even hypersonic range. This high speed allows the system to intercept fast-moving aerial targets, including ballistic missiles and supersonic aircraft.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Creating or sharing deepfakes of an individual without their permission can be considered as an offence under which of the following?
1. The Copyright Act, 1957
2. Indian Penal Code (IPC)
3. The Information Technology Act, 2000
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Explanation:
● Deepfakes, which are artificially generated media using machine learning algorithms, often involve manipulated images or videos. Although Indian laws do not explicitly mention deepfake technology, various legal provisions are available to address its misuse.
● Deepfake images often incorporate copyrighted material without proper authorisation. The Copyright Act, 1957 safeguards the rights of original content creators. If deepfake creators use copyrighted materials without permission, they can be held liable for copyright infringement. So, point 1 is correct.
● Deepfake images used to portray individuals falsely and harm their reputation can lead to defamation claims under the Indian Penal Code (IPC) potentially resulting in imprisonment and fines. So, point 2 is correct.
● The creation and distribution of deepfake images that invade an individual's privacy can be legally implicated under Indian law. Information Technology Act, 2000 can be invoked when deepfakes are employed to breach an individual's privacy or disseminate explicit content without their consent. So, point 3 is correct.
○ The creation and distribution of deepfake images may involve hacking, data breaches, or other cybersecurity violations. Unauthorised access to personal images or videos often breaches data protection laws and these breaches can be legally implicated under the Information Technology Act, 2000.
○ The use of deepfake images for fraudulent activities or identity theft can be prosecuted under several sections of the IPC and the Information Technology Act, 2000. For instance, the provisions related to Punishment for cheating by personation and Punishment for cheating by personation using a computer resource of the IT Act can be invoked against individuals engaged in such activities.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Termites are considered natural sources of methane.
Statement-II: The breakdown of the cellulose-rich diet of termites by gut microorganisms releases methane.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
Termites:
● They are counted among natural sources of methane (a greenhouse gas more potent than carbon dioxide) like wetlands, wild animals, livestock and geological features like volcanoes. They are estimated to account for 1-3 percent of methane emissions globally. So, statement 1 is correct.
● In natural ecosystems, they feed on and recycle the nutrients present in dead and decaying plant and animal matter. It is this cellulose-rich diet that causes their emissions. Methanogenic microorganisms that live in the gut of termites break down the cellulose entering the body and release methane. So, statement 2 is correct.
● In the 2000s, however, it was found that termites undo the damage partially by depositing in the mound a select group of microbes that can capture some emissions. Final emission depends on the balance between methane-emitting bacteria in the termites’ gut and methane-consuming bacteria in the mounds. So, statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Green Credit Programme (GCP):
1. Individuals are not eligible to earn green credits.
2. Green credits can be traded on a domestic market platform.
3. Any activity generating green credits is not eligible to receive carbon credits under the Carbon Credit Trading scheme.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The Green Credit Programme (GCP) aims to incentivise environmentally conscious practices and promote a sustainable lifestyle through a market-based mechanism.
● The Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE), an autonomous organisation under the Union Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change (MoEF&CC), is responsible for effectively implementing the GCP, including its management and operation.
● As per the scheme, individuals, industries, Farmers Producer’s Organisations (FPOs), Urban Local Bodies (ULB), Gram Panchayat and the private sector, among a host of other entities, will be able to earn green credit for undertaking environment-friendly actions. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● Green credits generated through such actions can be traded on a domestic market platform. These green credits will be over and above the current discourse of carbon credits. So, statement 2 is correct. ○ The green credits generated or procured by industries, companies and other entities to fulfil any legal obligation cannot be traded.
● Any activity generating green credits under the GCP will also receive carbon credits under the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme if it leads to the reduction or removal of carbon emissions. So, statement 3 is not correct. So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t: It is located at the junction of the Indian Sub-Continent Biogeography region and the Indo-China Biogeography Region. It is located between the Dapha Bum ridge of Mishmi Hills and the Patkai Ranges of the North Eastern Himalayas. The Yobin community lives in and around this Protected Area. This is the most likely description of:

so.t:
● Namdapha National Park in Arunachal Pradesh consists of evergreen Forests, Moist deciduous forests, sub-tropical forests, Temperate Forests and Alpine. The Namdapha protected area is wedged between the Dapha Bum ridge of Mishmi Hills, of North Eastern Himalayas and the Patkai Ranges.
● The Yobin community is living in and around the 1,985 sq km Namdapha National Park and Tiger Reserve.
● It is located at the junction of the Indian Sub-Continent Biogeography region and the Indo-China Biogeography Region.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Small Hydro Power Plants (SHP) in India:
1. Hydro Power plants with a capacity of 25 MW or below are classified as SHP Projects.
2. They are regulated by the Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
3. They are established by the State Government.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Hydro Power projects are classified as large and small hydro projects based on their sizes. Different countries have different size criteria to classify Small Hydro Power projects. In India, Hydro Power plants with a capacity of 25 MW or below are classified as Small Hydro Power Plants (SHP). So, statement 1 is correct.
● Hydro Power was looked after by the Union Ministry of Power before 1989. In 1989, plant capacity up to 3MW and below was transferred to the Union Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). So, statement 2 is not correct.
● Electricity and electricity generated from hydro projects is a subject in the concurrent list, and subject of both the Union and State Governments. However, water being a subject in the State List, the Small Hydro Power (SHP) projects are governed by State policies. The decision of setting up SHP projects or its allotment is taken by the State Government. So, statement 3 is correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t: Salamanders are a type of:

so.t:
● Salamanders are a group of amphibians typically characterised by their lizard-like appearance, with slender bodies, blunt snouts, short limbs projecting at right angles to the body, and the presence of a tail in both larvae and adults.
● All ten extant salamander families are grouped under the order Urodela from the group Caudata. Salamander diversity is highest in eastern North America, especially in the Appalachian Mountains; most species are found in the Holarctic realm, with some species present in the Neotropical realm.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

so.t:
Mangroves are salt-tolerant vegetation that grows in intertidal regions of rivers and estuaries in tropical and subtropical regions. They are referred to as ‘tidal forests’ and belong to the category of ‘tropical wetland rainforest ecosystem’. They are characterised by halophytic (salt loving) trees, shrubs and other plants growing in brackish to saline tidal waters.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding Depot Darshan Portal:
Statement I: It provides for a star-based rating for warehouses across the country.
Statement II: It allows for independent third-party audits for the Depots.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Depot Darpan Portal is an initiative of the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution, Government of India. with the objective to ensure that the Food Storage depots meet the highest quality and performance standards. It enables Depot managers to evaluate infrastructure, operational and financial performance on a near real-time basis
● Depot Darpan would create a seamless digital monitoring ecosystem that includes CCTV surveillance and IoT sensors to monitor parameters such as carbon dioxide and phosphine levels, fire hazards, humidity, unauthorized entry and temperature in real-time, thereby ensuring security and efficiency in food grain storage.
● The government plans to assess warehouses based on two categories, namely, infrastructural aspects and operational efficiency. The infra assessment includes safety, storage conditions, technological adoption, and statutory parameters. Operational efficiency includes stock turnover, losses, space utilization, manpower expenses, and profitability. Each category is evaluated independently, and the warehouse receives a Star rating based on the composite scoring from both parameters. So, statement I is correct.
● Depot Managers could upload geo-tagged inputs of the infrastructure available in their depot, generating automated ratings and action points for timely improvements, on this portal. The system ensures 100% validation by the supervisory officers and random third-party audits. So, statement II is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is the correct explanation for Statement I
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is not the correct explanation for Statement I
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to ‘polar nights’:
Statement I: All latitudes situated in polar circles experience total darkness for six months.
Statement II: The rotation of the Earth with respect to the Sun results in polar nights.
Which one of the following is correct?

so.t:
● The polar night is the term for when the night lasts for more than 24 hours inside the polar circles. In this case, ‘night’ is defined as when the centre of the Sun is below the horizon.
● Not all latitudes are situated north enough to experience sustained total darkness; instead, their brightest moments are levels of polar twilight that occur in the early afternoon before evening approaches and the darkness intensifies. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The polar night is caused by the rotation of the earth in relation to the position of the sun. The earth rotates on a tilted axis of around 23.5 degrees. As a result of this axial tilt, there are periods of the year when the Arctic Circle and the Antarctic Circle are either completely exposed or obscured from the sun. So, statement 2 is correct.
● When they are obscured, it causes the prolonged darkness known as the polar night, while when they are exposed, it creates a prolonged period of daylight known as the Midnight Sun, or a “polar day,”. If polar nights take place from September to March, the Midnight Sun comes out from April to August. So, statement – I is incorrect but Statement – II is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

opt.t: These are narrow bands of strong winds that usually blow from west to east.
They are formed when warm air masses meet the cold air masses in the atmosphere.
The jet streams exist in the mid to upper troposphere.
Airplanes cannot fly in the jet streams.

q.t: Which one of the following statements is not correct with reference to jet streams?

so.t:
● Jet streams are narrow bands of strong wind that generally blow from west to east all across the globe. They are formed when warm air masses meet cold air masses in the atmosphere. Earth has four primary jet streams: two polar jet streams, near the north and south poles and two subtropical jet streams closer to the equator.
● Jet streams are located about eight to fourteen kilometres above Earth’s surface in the mid to upper troposphere.
● Airplanes also fly in the mid to upper troposphere. So, if an Airplane flies in a powerful jet stream and they are travelling in the same direction, the airplane can get a boost. That’s why an airplane flying a route from west to east can generally make the trip faster than an airplane travelling the same route east to west.
Therefore, option (d) is the answer.

q.t:
Consider the following States:
1. Andhra Pradesh
2. Assam
3. Gujarat
4. Uttar Pradesh
5. Arunachal Pradesh
How many of the above are generally known as tobacco-producing States?

so.t:
● Tobacco is one of the most economically significant agricultural crops in the world. It is a drought tolerant, hardy and short-duration crop which can be grown on soils where other crops cannot be cultivated profitably.
● India is the 2nd largest producer and exporter of tobacco after China and Brazil respectively. In the global scenario, Indian tobacco accounts for 10% of the area and 9% of the total production.
● Key tobacco-producing States in India include Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka, West Bengal, Telangana and Bihar. So, points 1, 3 and 4 are correct.
○ Rice is the predominant crop in Assam along with some other important crops like pulses, jute, tea, sugarcane, potato, cotton, oil seeds, fruits crops, coconut, arecanut etc. So, point 2 is not correct.
○ The topography and climate of Arunachal Pradesh is conducive for the cultivation of rice, millet, wheat, pulses, sugarcane and potatoes. So, point 4 is not correct.
● Gujarat is the leading producer of tobacco across India followed by Andhra Pradesh. The overall tobacco production across the country was about 755 million metric tons. So, only three of the above are generally known as tobacco-producing states.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Well-drained loam soil is the most suitable for soybean plants.
2. Import of genetically modified (GM) soybeans is banned in India.
3. Telangana is the highest soybean-producing state.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● The Soybeans are nitrogen-fixing legume crops. They are grown under warm conditions in the tropics, subtropics and temperate climates. They are relatively resistant to low and very high temperatures. The ideal rainfall range is between 500 mm and 1000 mm. Well-drained and fertile loam soils with a pH between 6.0 and 7.5 are most suitable for the cultivation of soybeans. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Crushed genetically modified soybean is a major ingredient of poultry feed. It is a rich source of protein. In 2022, the Government of India approved the import of genetically modified (GM) soybeans. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● Maharashtra is placed at number one in soybean production in India ahead of Madhya Pradesh. So, statement 3 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Arrange the following States in the correct descending order based on their length of coastline:
1. Maharashtra
2. Gujarat
3. Andhra Pradesh
4. Tamil Nadu

so.t:
● India has a coastline of 8118 km, covering 9 maritime States/4 Union Territories and providing livelihood support to millions of coastal fisher folk.
● Gujarat forms the longest coastline of all Indian States. The total length of the coastline of Gujarat is 1214.7 Km. Some of the important ports of Gujarat are Alang, Kandla, Mandavi, Okha and Porbandar.
● The coastal length of Andhra Pradesh is 973.7 Km which makes it the 2nd longest coastline State of India. Its Major Ports are Visakhapatnam, Kakinada and Machilipatnam.
● The coastal length of Tamil Nadu is 906.9 Km which makes it the 3rd longest coastline State of India. The major sea ports of Tamil Nadu are Chennai port and Tuticorin Port Trust.
● Coastal length of Maharashtra is 652.6 Km which makes it the 4th longest coastline State of India. Some important ports in Maharashtra – Jawaharlal Nehru Port, Mumbai Port Trust, Dharamtar Port, Mora port, Redi Port, Jaitapur and Rewas port.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the statements with reference to Tea plantations in India:
1. Tea was introduced by the British in Assam as there were no native tea species in India.
2. The Tea Committee was established in 1834 under the chairmanship of William Bentinck.
3. India was exporting more tea to Britain than China towards the end of the 19th Century.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The ‘discovery’ of the native variant of tea in Assam brought about a new quest for the origins of the tea plant in India. English botanists thought that tea was native to India and British production of tea in India was a way of bringing back what was originally Indian. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● After the Charter Act of 1833, the East India Company lost its trade monopoly with China. The Tea Committee was established in 1834, under William Bentinck, the then Governor-General of India. Also in 1834, Charles Gutzlaff and George Gordon were commissioned by the firm, Jardine Matheson, to undertake an opium voyage to China. While there, they were also expected to extract tea seeds, techniques and resources from the Chinese. The mission was not quite successful. So, statement 2 is correct.
● In 1853, Fraser’s Magazine suggested that India and not China was the “natural home of the tea plant”. By 1888, Indian tea exports into Britain were over 86 million pounds, exceeding China’s 80 million pounds. Thus, India was exporting more tea to Britain than China towards the end of the 19th Century. So, statement 3 is correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding PM SHRI Scheme:
I. It is a Central Sector Scheme.
II. It is aimed at upgrading existing schools with state-of-the-art facilities in States and Union Territories.
III. The schools under this scheme will be developed in line with the principles of New Education Policy 2020.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● The Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India (PM-SHRI) scheme is an initiative by the Ministry of Education, Government of India, to upgrade existing schools into model schools aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. These schools will serve as exemplars for holistic, inclusive, and high-quality education.
● The PM-SHRI scheme is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme. For the execution and implementation of the scheme both the Centre and States/UTs contribute funds, typically in a 60:40 ratio (90:10 for NE states and UTs). So, statement I is not correct.
● PM-SHRI focuses on upgrading existing schools under the respective State Government or the Union Territory administration. The scheme enhances current infrastructure, pedagogy, and inclusivity to create model schools. So, statement II is correct.
● A core goal of the scheme is to implement NEP 2020 principles like experiential learning, competency-based curriculum, and technology integration. Schools under PM-SHRI will serve as examples of NEP-aligned education. So, statement III is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t: ‘Chlorpyrifros’ which was recently talked about in news, belongs to which of the following class of chemicals:

so.t:
● Chlorpyrifos is an organophosphorus pesticide well known for its extensive use in the agricultural sector for the purposes of killing insects, worms and a variety of other pests. High use of chlorpyrifos results in poisoning to non-target species like humans.
● Chlorpyrifos is widely used in developing countries for effective crop protection because of its potential toxicity against a broad spectrum of insect species and it does not affect the seed activity leaving little residue on food grain, despite being cost effective.
● It is neurotoxic to animals and blocks the muscle conduction, eventually leading to paralysis and death. It is also linked to adverse effects on neurodevelopment, reduced birth size, lung and prostate cancer upon chronic exposure. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

Please read the following instructions very carefully:

1. You have 120 minutes to complete the test.
2. The test contains a total of 100 questions And 200 Marks.
3. There is only one correct answer to each question. Click on the most appropriate option to mark it as your answer.
4. You will be awarded 2 for each correct answer.
5. There is no penalty for wrong answer.
6. You can change your answer by clicking on some other option.
7. You can unmark your answer by clicking on the “Clear Response” button.
8. A Number list of all questions appears at the right hand side of the screen. You can access the questions in any order within a section or across sections by clicking on the question number given on the number list.
9. You can use rough sheets while taking the test. Do not use calculators, log tables, dictionaries, or any other printed/online reference material during the test.
10. Do not click the button “Submit test” before completing the test. A test once submitted cannot be resumed.
